text: Ilya Sutskever — {{c1::We're moving}}(hint) from the age of scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever You know {{c2::what’s crazy}}(hint)? Dwarkesh Patel {{c3::Meaning what}}(hint)?
original: Ilya Sutskever — We're moving from the age of scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever You know what’s crazy? Dwarkesh Patel Meaning what?

text: Ilya Sutskever {{c1::Don’t you think}}(hint) so? {{c2::All this AI}}(hint) stuff and {{c3::all this Bay Area}}(hint)… that it’s happening.
original: Ilya Sutskever Don’t you think so? All this AI stuff and all this Bay Area… that it’s happening.

text: {{c1::Isn’t it straight}}(hint) {{c2::out of science}}(hint) fiction? Dwarkesh Patel Another thing that’s crazy is {{c3::how normal}}(hint) {{c4::the slow takeoff}}(hint) feels.
original: Isn’t it straight out of science fiction? Dwarkesh Patel Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels.

text: The idea that {{c1::we’d be investing}}(hint) 1% of GDP in AI, {{c2::I feel like}}(hint) {{c3::it would have}}(hint) felt like {{c4::a bigger deal}}(hint), whereas {{c5::right now}}(hint) {{c6::it just feels}}(hint)... Ilya Sutskever
original: The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, I feel like it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas right now it just feels... Ilya Sutskever

text: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things {{c1::pretty fast}}(hint), {{c2::it turns out}}(hint). But also it’s {{c3::kind of abstract}}(hint).
original: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things pretty fast, it turns out. But also it’s kind of abstract.

text: Dwarkesh Patel {{c1::Should we actually begin}}(hint) here? I think this is an interesting discussion.
original: Dwarkesh Patel Should we actually begin here? I think this is an interesting discussion.

text: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend {{c1::dollar amount of investment}}(hint). {{c2::I don’t think}}(hint) anyone knows what to do with that.
original: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend dollar amount of investment. I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.

text: I think the {{c1::impact of AI}}(hint) is going to be felt. AI is going to be {{c2::diffused through the economy}}(hint).
original: I think the impact of AI is going to be felt. AI is going to be diffused through the economy.

text: There’ll be {{c1::very strong economic forces}}(hint) for this, and I think the impact is going to be {{c2::felt very strongly}}(hint). Dwarkesh Patel When do you {{c3::expect that impact}}(hint)?
original: There’ll be very strong economic forces for this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?

text: I think the {{c1::models seem smarter}}(hint) than their economic impact would imply. Ilya Sutskever
original: I think the models seem smarter than their economic impact would imply. Ilya Sutskever

text: This is one of the {{c1::very confusing things}}(hint) about {{c2::the models right now}}(hint). How to {{c3::reconcile the fact}}(hint) that they are {{c4::doing so well}}(hint) on evals?
original: This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals?

text: {{c1::How to reconcile the fact}}(hint) that they are {{c2::doing so well}}(hint) on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are {{c3::pretty hard evals}}(hint).”
original: How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.”

text: They are {{c1::doing so well}}(hint). But the {{c2::economic impact seems}}(hint) to be {{c3::dramatically behind}}(hint).
original: They are doing so well. But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind.

text: An example would be, {{c1::let’s say}}(hint) you {{c2::use vibe coding}}(hint) to do something. You go to some place and then you {{c3::get a bug}}(hint).
original: An example would be, let’s say you use vibe coding to do something. You go to some place and then you get a bug.

text: Then you {{c1::tell the model}}(hint), “{{c2::Can you please fix}}(hint) the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. {{c3::Let me go fix}}(hint) that.”
original: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me go fix that.”

text: Then you tell it, “{{c1::You have this new second bug}}(hint),” and it tells you, “Oh my God, {{c2::how could I have done it}}(hint)? You’re {{c3::so right again}}(hint),”
original: Then you tell it, “You have this new second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re so right again,”
target_phrases: You have this new second bug
how could I have done it

text: and brings back the first bug, and you {{c1::can alternate between}}(hint) those.
original: and brings back the first bug, and you can alternate between those.

text: I have {{c1::two possible explanations}}(hint). The more {{c2::whimsical explanation}}(hint) is that maybe {{c3::RL training makes}}(hint) the models a little too {{c4::single-minded and narrowly focused}}(hint), {{c5::a little bit too unaware}}(hint), even though it also {{c6::makes them aware}}(hint) in some other ways.
original: I have two possible explanations. The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.

text: Because of this, they {{c1::can’t do basic things}}(hint). But there is another explanation.
original: Because of this, they can’t do basic things. But there is another explanation.

text: But there is another explanation. Back when people were {{c1::doing pre-training}}(hint), the {{c2::question of what data}}(hint) to {{c3::train on was answered}}(hint), {{c4::because that answer}}(hint) was everything.
original: But there is another explanation. Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything.

text: But when people do {{c1::RL training}}(hint), they do {{c2::need to think}}(hint). They say, “Okay, we want to have {{c3::this kind of RL training}}(hint) for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
original: But when people do RL training, they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have this kind of RL training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”

text: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just {{c1::produce new RL environments}}(hint) and just {{c2::add it to the training mix}}(hint). The question is, well, what are those?
original: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just add it to the training mix. The question is, well, what are those?
target_phrases: add it to the training mix

text: There are so many {{c1::degrees of freedom}}(hint). There is such a huge {{c2::variety of RL environments}}(hint) {{c3::you could produce}}(hint).
original: There are so many degrees of freedom. There is such a huge variety of RL environments you could produce.

text: All this AI stuff and {{c1::all this Bay Area}}(hint)… that it’s happening. Isn’t it {{c2::straight out of science fiction}}(hint)?
original: All this AI stuff and all this Bay Area… that it’s happening. Isn’t it straight out of science fiction?

text: It means that you see it in the news, that {{c1::such and such company}}(hint) {{c2::announced such and such}}(hint) dollar amount. That’s all you see. It’s not really felt in any other way so far.
original: It means that you see it in the news, that such and such company announced such and such dollar amount. That’s all you see. It’s not really felt in any other way so far.

text: Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the average person’s point of view {{c1::nothing is that different}}(hint), will continue being true even into the singularity. Ilya Sutskever No, I don’t think so. Dwarkesh Patel Okay, interesting.
original: Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the average person’s point of view nothing is that different, will continue being true even into the singularity. Ilya Sutskever No, I don’t think so. Dwarkesh Patel Okay, interesting.

text: how can the model, {{c1::on the one hand}}(hint), do these amazing things, and then {{c2::on the other hand}}(hint), {{c3::repeat itself twice}}(hint) in some situation?
original: how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
